text: Mid-level backend engineer with three years of professional experience. Comfortable with Python and Postgres for typical web-app workloads. Applying to senior roles because I am ambitious and ready to grow, even if the role is a stretch.

Currently a Software Engineer at Bookstack (Series A, ~40 employees, B2B SaaS). I have been here for two and a half years and was recently promoted from junior to mid-level. I work on the API and integrations team.

Shipped the Stripe billing integration at Bookstack. Wrote the webhook handlers and the subscription state machine on top of Stripe's APIs. This is the closest I have come to working on payments, although Stripe abstracts most of the hard parts.
Python: three years of production experience. Have worked with Django, FastAPI, and Celery. Comfortable with the language but I am still learning the deeper performance and async corners.

Postgres: three years of experience as a consumer of the database. Have written reasonably complex queries and added indexes when ORM-generated queries were slow. Have not run Postgres in production myself.

Distributed systems: limited direct experience. Have used Redis as a queue and Celery for background jobs but have not designed a system that needs strict idempotency or exactly-once semantics. Eager to learn.
Kafka: no production experience. I have read the documentation and built a toy producer-consumer in a weekend project but have not run Kafka under real load.
First job out of college was as a Junior Engineer at Pageturn, a small ed-tech company. Worked there for six months on the front-end before moving to backend at Bookstack.
On-call: Bookstack does not have a formal on-call rotation. The senior engineers handle pages. I have helped triage a few incidents during business hours but have never been the primary responder.
BS in Mathematics from a state university, 2022. Took a few computer science classes but most of my software skills are self-taught and on-the-job.

Motivation for applying: I want to work on something with high stakes where correctness matters more than speed of feature output. Payments feels like the right environment to grow into a real senior engineer.
